sentences.text: Hello?
Hi, how are you?
Good, how are you?
I'm doing well, thanks.
Thanks for taking the time to give us the demo.
Of course!
How's your day going so far?
It's Monday.
It's always crazy on a Monday.
It's property management.
How's yours?
About the same, yeah.
Not the same kind of crazy, because I'm not… I'm on the other side of property management, but… It's always… Mondays are always my busiest days for meetings.
It's like, Thursday, Friday, no one wants to meet, but Monday, everyone's like, let's just go.
Everybody's like, oh, we want to be part of it, we want to be part of it.
Do you know if anyone else on your end is joining?
Yeah, Desiree and Lorenzo are both gonna hop on.
Okay.
Cool.
Well, we can wait a little bit, then, to let them join.
Yeah, I just had actually just emailed both of them, and I said, hey, are we still meeting today?
There's Lorenzo right now.
Hello, everybody, how's it going?
Speak of the devil.
Good, how are you?
Good, I am doing good.
Well, is your day going so far?
It has been swell.
We are just getting… tuned into these afternoon meetings, and getting back into it.
How about you guys'?
Same, same.
It's like….
duty right now, so I'm working.
from us.
sister's house, and I have daycare drop-off, daycare pickup.
I don't have any of my own, so sometimes it's fun playing house with them.
There you go, that is fun.
As long as you don't lose any of them or forget any of them.
There's only two, so not too hard to count them, but you never know.
We did a splash pad yesterday, that was quite the experience.
There we go, I love it.
Yep.
Are we gonna give Desiree a couple minutes, or do you want me to get started?
I'm okay if we get started, I mean… Yeah, let's go ahead and get started.
Pardon.
be making the decision, so yeah, I'm okay.
And it doesn't really knows the package, so….
Yep.
Yeah, she's seen, this probably 4 times now, so, she can jump in whenever she.
Yeah.
Cool, I'll just get… I guess I'll get started with, have you guys seen a demo of this product before?
How familiar are you?
If at all?
Otherwise, I can just start.
Not at all.
Brand new to me.
Okay.
Awesome.
Well, happy to show you and chat with you guys a little bit about it.
Looks like Desiree just joined.
There we go.
Hello?
Hi there, sorry about that.
It kept saying I had an invalid meeting ID for some reason.
-Oh.
No worries at all.
We were just chatting about the weekend and playing babysitter, and now we're gonna jump in, so….
Okay, great.

Cool, well, sounds like you guys are not super familiar with the product, so, I'll start… I was thinking to start with, like, the importance of the Google profiles, why we've had so many customers sign on, and really what they're saying their biggest use cases are.
And then give a product overview.

So I can go through a little bit here, and then I thought it might make sense to just show a couple properties that are live on this product, so you can see exactly what, like, the end result looks like in real time.
And then I did include a couple reporting examples as well.
So, I can show what that looks like and what, Cushman & Wakefield specifically is seeing as far as analytics from this.
Is there anything else you guys wanted me to cover today?
Anything else you wanted to add to the agenda?
No, I was just kind of hoping to see, which arenas of that Google business profile.
Maven kind of touches, and then how that differs from any other products on the market.
I know we use, Zumper at some of our sites, I don't know if there's a little bit of overlap, no overlap, lots of overlap, just kind of hearing about that stuff.
Yep.
Do you have Zumper on all of your properties?
We have them on 3 or 4 of our Arizona sites.
Tammy, we have it at all of ours too, don't we?
Born in California.
Yeah, so we're probably on Zumper at, 8 or 9 of our sites total.
Okay.
Okay.
And about how many properties do you guys oversee?
17, and you've got Zumper on a little less than half of them.
Yeah, something like that.
About half, okay.
Okay.
Yeah.

And just to add on, sorry, the Zemper tool that we are using at, the Tower 16 assets, it… it's a review removal tool that they… that they have, so it takes down any negative reviews if… if they're able to challenge it through Google.
So I don't know if there's any way for us to maybe partner with them with this tool, or if you have any information on that.
That would be great to hear.
Okay, yeah, I'll get started with, like, the purposes behind this and use cases, and then I'll show the live properties.

There is some overlap, we do compete against Zumper, but I'll show you the differences and what customers that have switched from Zumper to us have said they like, and then you can kind of compare the two.
That's perfect.
Okay, sounds good.
Cool, well, jumping in, so the… the products that we have, we have a couple different products under the Maven AI name.
We have our Google Business Automation tool, and then we have an automated social posting tool as well.
So I know that we've been mostly working with the Cushman team on the Google Business, product specifically.
So, all the products that we have are built to automate processes.
So, a lot of use cases that customers say is saving their team's time from posting.
But then also fully optimizing the profiles to get more eyes on your profiles, get more clicks to your websites.
That's a huge use case of this tool.
But really, it's all to automate the processes.
So, we directly, syndicate to your property management softwares.
So it's like the source of truth for all of the information that we would be pulling over.
this piece, Zumper does have, so they have the syndication to the feeds as well.

But the biggest differences that you're gonna see throughout this is The quality of the posts, every post that we post will have photos, we're highlighting amenities in pretty much every post that we have.
But really, this tool is built to feed what Google's looking for.
And what type of content is going to help your properties rank higher, get more eyes on your properties.
So, Google tells us that the Google Business Profile itself has the highest influence, on your local SEO overall.

And then we know with the increasing, reliance on AI overviews, we are fully optimizing this content to match what the AI overview is like, and what type of content they're looking for, which is super relevant with, like, the Q&A that we have, … But then we know Google also rewards on a few different factors, one of them being relevancy.
They want the content that you're posting to your profiles to be relevant to your business, so your property.
They look at the frequency, how often you're posting, how often you're making updates, and then the uniqueness of the content.
So, are you posting the same thing over and over?
Are you posting the same thing at multiple properties, or is the content truly unique to this Google profile?
So, because we know all of that, we built this tool to fit all of this, and this tool is built in-house.
So I know the tool that Zumper uses is a white label, so they're using someone else for this type of product.
So that's relevant because it gives us the ability to customize it a bit further.
So we can get your post sounding like your property using your brand voice, and it gives us the ability to customize the content that we're posting a bit more.
So, I'll jump into the product itself.
I know you said you wanted to see exactly where on the profile, this tool posts.
So, the overview is that we are posting every day to your profiles.
We are posting in the products feed, so it's live pricing and availability at the floor plan level.
And again, I'll show you what this looks like.
We're also pulling concessions directly from your feed to your profiles.
And then we automate the Q&A section as well.
I will show you some examples of what this looks like live.
We'll start with the posts.
So, I know with the Zumber product, they do, posts as well.
I would say the differences that I've heard customers point out the most is that, every day, the post will have a photo with it.
And the content is built to match the photo.
So, we're not gonna be posting about your… front entranceway or your lobby, and call out the pool.
So the content is really built to fit what Google's looking for, but then also for the posts to make sense together.
Within the post, one other thing that I think would be unique here is that we can insert SEO keywords throughout the post.
So what we can essentially do here is take a list of SEO keywords that either you're already ranking highly on, or you want to rank higher on.
We insert those on the backend, and then the posts that you see will include that verbiage.
So we've had a lot of customers use this for highlighting… we're already including the city name, but, like, neighborhoods, that we're not just getting from the feed.
We can highlight those here.
If there's something unique about your property, like it's near a university or a football stadium, I've seen this used in a few different ways.
So, really just different ways to differentiate how this is sounding, but then also feed the algorithm and what Google's looking for.
And here's an example of one of those concession posts.
So, like everything else, we're pulling this information directly from the feed to your profile.
We know that a lot of time and effort goes into creating these concessions, so this is just one more place we can be advertising it.
This post looks a little different.
It's advertised as an offer, so we do that, so this is a dynamic field.
As soon as this concession is expired, or it's removed from your feed, this post will go away, it'll fall off.
So, we're not storing any outdated concessions, this is more of an always-updated kind of post.
Great.
Any questions on that, on the posts?
So the managers have access… oh, sorry, Lorenzo.
No, go ahead, Deb.
So the managers will have access, like, a login to do all of those… posts and everything, or how is that, or will… is there so many logins given if I needed to go in, or how does that work?
So the tool is… the team doesn't have to log in to create anything.
So the posts are all created with the AI tool.
So we're getting all the information from the feed, and then layering on top the AI tool, our… we're manually creating these posts.
Now, you do have… there is a full, like, dashboard experience with this as well, so if you wanted your team to have control over this, they could always log into the backend, the dashboard.
In here, they can see all the upcoming posts.
So, you can toggle this by property.
If you just wanted them to have access to one property, you can do… we can do that.
And then, this would be relevant if you guys were looking at our social tool as well, but for the sake of this, this would just show all of the upcoming automated Google posts.
And then if they ever wanted to make changes, swap the photo out, or completely deny the post, they can do that here, and then this would regenerate a post in the same day.

I will say we… we have some customers that love using this feature, and they love to log in and see what's coming, so they can plan any sort of, like, community-level posts that they're doing, but we have a lot of customers that don't log in because the tool is built to run on its own.
So, totally optional if you wanted your teams to log into this.
But the tool is built to automate the full process so that your team doesn't have to log in.
Perfect, okay.
That's what I was wondering, like, is it grab… it was just grabbing from the data that's already… that we've inputted into the system.
Yeah, directly from the feed.
So there's no additional work that you need to do other than, have your feed updated.
So we're pulling photos, amenity-based information, pricing availability, all from the feed.
No work really has to be done.
for implementation, it's, granting us access to the Google profiles, and then turning on the feed syndication, just like you would for an ILS, and then our team takes it over from there.
Okay, thank you.
And Tammy, for our other assets that we've, implemented this tool on, I have access, so I can go into the dashboard to say if they wanted to do, like, a custom post or anything like that.
I would have access to do that.
Perfect, my lifeline, Desiree, I love it.
Thank you.
Yeah.
Yes, that's the goal of this, is so that you don't have to log in to do it.

So, … There is the ability to manually post, that's what Desiree's talking about here, so we can turn this on if you wanted to do a manual post to Google, type the content, drag and drop the photo or the video, you can schedule it out.
So, that's all included with this.
And then going into the products feed.
So here is where we are displaying pricing and availability.

So the differences between the Zumper tool and ours is that this is… this is showing live pricing and availability, but it's at the floor plan level, and this is reflecting the lowest-priced floor plan.
So, if you had 3 zincs available right now, this would pull in the lowest-priced one, where I believe Zumper's is a range.
we're displaying the lowest price for SEO purposes, when people are looking up two-bedroom apartments in Phoenix for $2,300 or less.
That's why we have the lowest price displayed.
Yeah, and then just to clarify, Kylie, we don't have, Zemper listings.
It's, it's a different product, so we wouldn't have any, like, overlap.
The tool.
Right.
I think for the Tower 16 sites is just, like, a review removal tool, to boost their reputation scores.
Got it.
Okay, that makes sense.
I'll stop comparing the two, then.
That makes sense.
No, it's good to know, like, the differences, too.
Yep.
Yeah, so this would display, floor plan level pricing and availability, updated, 4 times a day, so it's going in and pinging the feed four times a day to display what's available.
So, more of a live feed.
And then also within the products feed, we are displaying, amenities.
So, just one more place that we can be pushing those amenities, pulling the amenity, fields directly from the feed, and then our tool can match it to the photo.
So, no work that needs to be done, no tagging of photos that needs to be done.
Our tool is doing the matching, to display what, amenities you have.
Any questions on that piece?
not….
for me, I think that's a great feature to have.
Yeah, I didn't even realize that there was a products feed with floor plan pricing.
Yeah, yeah, it's a bit newer, and when we found out about it, we're like, let's try and automate.
Yeah.
So, I think it gives a nice visual as well.
So, not only, obviously, we want to put these keywords everywhere we can, but as a prospect, I think it looks really nice, too.
Yeah.
Within each of these, you'll see the Visit Site button.
Same thing in here.
There's a link from every one of the posts as well.
So, the goal of this tool is to increase your exposure, optimize your profile so that we get more eyes on them, but also to increase traffic back to your websites.
So I'll show you what that report looks like, but that's a big use case of this, is increasing website traffic, increasing clicks to your pages.
Getting more impressions on the maps as well.
So, all of that is a, … End result of this.
The last area we are automating is the Q&A.
So, I've had a lot of customers over the last 4 or 5 months tell us that they're focusing more on, like, FAQ-type content on their property websites.
Main reasons being AI Overviews really likes that type of content.
This is the same thing, just getting it live on your profiles, on your Google profiles.
So, we've compiled, it's like 30 different question… lists of questions, some of the most, like, frequently asked Q&A questions.
So, around availability, around concessions, around amenities.
And we list them all here, same as everything else, we're posting it, and then our tool auto-answers it, and that's also updated every day.
So we know these questions will change because availability changes.
So, you can see as of yesterday, and that will update again today, we do have two bedrooms available.
Out of those 30-ish questions that I mentioned, we're matching them to your property?
So, what I mean by that is we're only asking… let's find a good example of one.
Does this property have a pool?
We're not going to ask that question if the property doesn't have a pool.
We're using these questions to highlight amenities you do have, and availability you do have.
Any questions on that piece?
Are you guys doing anything in your Q&A section right now, of your profiles?
No, only, like, the, you know, individual, I think, responses, but that's more of a review function, I think.
Yeah, yep.
Yeah, so this would be a good way, obviously, like everything else, push those amenity keywords that we've been talking about a lot.
mostly, an organic SEO play here, but also good to have this fully filled out.
No work that needs to be done on your end.
And if you have some older questions as well, we'll help push those down.
So… I think it makes it look a little bit nicer than having questions from 3 years ago, still hanging out… Yeah.
Hanging out in there.
Definitely.
Yeah.
Alright.
Well, we went through all of that.
The last thing that I wanted to share is performance.
So, like I said, this is a big use case of this tool, is getting more eyes on your profiles, getting more actions when people do land there.
So, this is just a quick snapshot of what results have been looking like.
So, this is for 65 properties, this is a sample size of 65 properties, and this is for the first 7 months.
So, we took… they started in January, we took the first 7 months compared to the same time period the previous year to account for seasonality.
And what they're seeing as far as increases go.
So, 10% increase in search impressions, 11% increase in website actions, 22% increase in map impressions, 22% increase in driving directions.
So, we can also further break that down by property.
So, you'll be able to see all of those KPIs broken down by property.
And that's just in the first 7 months.
So, we really see the best numbers happening between, like, 9 to 12 months, because it's an organic tool, so it takes some time to show really strong increases.
But this is what we're seeing, and this is a variation of properties as well.
There's some… you'll see some lease-ups in here, like this one, as Evora, that's a lease-up property.
So, you're seeing a 200% increase in search impressions.
But then you can see all of the other ones that are conventional properties that are still hanging out.
8%, so… Is this a way you think you would be measuring the tool, a tool like this?

… That's an excellent question, because the way that we would end up measuring, This one, we might not be able to, … actually get numbers for, because what we do is, when folks come in and sign leases, if they've found us just by a natural Google search.
… That would end up kind of flowing in… Desiree, when that hits, like, our actual marketing report, which one would that be?
Is that… ….
It's gonna probably….
Drive-by, or…?
No, it's probably gonna go under property website.
So, property website, exactly.
So, it would be trickier to track the success of this one.
It would increase property website, I guess, leases signed.
And then I'm just thinking about how we would be able to measure this outside of that.
But these metrics are certainly, certainly very helpful.
I think we're just exactly what you're kind of highlighting here, trying to focus on, improving that Google business profile, because that does drive
Probably upwards of, like, 90% or 95% of our property website traffic.
Yep, I hear that a lot.
I hear… and companies that we've been working with for a couple years even saying that those numbers are increasing every.
be here.
So… Yeah.
that's why we include this in here.
Maybe it's not the main way you measure it, but a lot of companies or properties like to see this, to help with the telling the story of the ROI.
Some just want their profiles fully optimized and filled out, and that's the end of it.
But, this is another way that we can… we can measure, too.
Cool.
Any questions that you guys have for me?
I don't think so.
When you guys, offer… I guess, how do the, review… kind of services and responses work.
Is that similar to Zumper's function, where we can kind of go in and challenge stuff, or is that just purely, like, responding to questions?
Yep.
We do have a reputation management tool as well.
We didn't go over any of that.
The Google tool and the social tool that I mentioned a little bit earlier, both of those tools are ours and built in-house.
We have a reputation tool and a listings management tool.
Very transparently, both of those we white-label BirdEye for.
Okay.
So, we could look at that as well, and look at bundling that together, if that's something you're interested in.
I'll say the… we do not have a review removal aspect of that tool.
So if that would be a make or break, we don't have that just yet.
But we have a full… the full dashboard with BirdEye for review management, review response, review solicitation, all of that's included.
Okay, … I guess, I'm just trying to think about, kind of, the overlap between, … those two.
So, from, I guess, kind of, like, our reputation score, is anything from, Maven… Is that mainly, I guess, just responding to kind of comments that are on that Google Business profile?
Yeah, so the Google tool will not interfere or interact at all with the reviews tool.
So if you were continuing to keep on with the Zumper tool, this wouldn't impact or have any… any overlap at all.
Gotcha, okay.
I just checked right now, so it looks like the majority of your properties are on Sochi for review response, so, yeah, there wouldn't be any overlap with the Maven AI tool.
Okay.
I forgot about Sochi, because we are using that at a bunch of our sites.
Yep, so… Alright.
Any other questions?
I don't think so.
Pricing for this one, is it on a per unit per month basis?
We are… it is per property per month.
Per property per month, okay.
Yeah.
Okay.
Yeah, and it's… I think right now you're offering the $99 per month, right, Kylie?
And then the first… or the first 2 months free?
Yeah, yeah, right now we'd be looking at an October start.
Okay.
If that works for you guys.
So it would be… we'd be able to start implementation… where implementation's about 5 business days, so pretty simple, turning the two things on on your end, and then our team takes it over.
Pushing the invoicing start to October 1, so you'd get the first month.
5 weeks free.
Okay, just because I'm writing this down, I have, like, a big solutions log.

So, Maven is our Google business profile kind of solution, where that's the one that does the posts, the products, and just kind of helps you organically come up higher, because your business looks really good to Google from kind of Google's Internal metrics.
And then, what was your reputation management one?
Did you say that was bird's eye?
Yeah, I feel like you just explained that better than I did.
Yes, we do, BirdEye is the tool that we use for our.
Okay.
Okay.
Could we set up a demo on that one, too?
Wonderful.
Yep.
Yep, we can do that.
I can send you an email after this with some times, if that works?
Yes.
Cool.
So that's reputation management.
Okay, wonderful.
That is all of the actual questions I got, Kylie.
Thank you.
That's super helpful, and I honestly didn't even know about the, those posts or the products section of the Google Business, but that's definitely, going to impact how you're coming up on those.
For sure.
Well, I'm glad we were able to connect as well.
Thank you for coordinating this, Desiree.
Of course.
Thank you, Kylie.
Of course.
Wonderful.
Thank you.
Thanks, everybody.
Lorenzo, do you have a minute, that I can give you a call?
I'm hopping directly onto another one, Tammy, I have probably two and a half minutes.
Okay, I'll talk to you later, then.
Thanks.
